title: Dispatch perf rework: pipeline cache + device-local buffers + staging
goal: Rework VulkanContext::dispatch so real-GPU numbers reflect kernel math, not M1.5 per-call setup overhead. Currently saxpy_1m on RTX PRO 6000 takes 34 ms (should be ~50 µs) due to per-call pipeline compilation + host-visible buffers going through PCIe BAR. This milestone cuts that to sub-ms.
scope_in: Persistent pipeline cache on VulkanContext: keyed by (spirv_hash, binding_plan_hash, push_constant_size); reuses OpModule + descriptor_set_layout + pipeline_layout + compute_pipeline across dispatches with same kernel

New API `VulkanContext::prepare_kernel(spirv, binding_plan) -> KernelHandle` — compile once, dispatch many. Existing `dispatch()` keeps one-shot compatibility but internally routes through prepare_kernel.

Device-local buffers with staging: compute SSBOs allocated DEVICE_LOCAL; HOST_VISIBLE staging buffer for upload; readback via HOST_VISIBLE staging + vkCmdCopyBuffer; pipeline barriers between COPY→COMPUTE and COMPUTE→COPY
Buffer reuse: staging and device-local buffers cached by size class within the KernelHandle; subsequent dispatches with same shape reuse allocations

Pipeline cache on disk (optional): vk::PipelineCache created with `vkCreatePipelineCache(.., data_from_file)` at context init, serialized to `~/.cache/axc/pipeline.cache` at context drop. Accelerates repeated cold starts.
New bench baselines on the reworked path: both Lavapipe and real GPU should show dramatic speedup on the 1M benches
Regression gate auto-reblessed on M2.3a merge (the old baselines are intentionally invalidated)
Correctness preserved: saxpy + vector_add still produce bit-exact CPU-reference output
Semantics preserved: no change to emitted SPIR-V, no change to HIR, no change to driver API surface
DispatchError gains 2 new variants: PipelineCacheLoadFailed, StagingCopyFailed
scope_out: Dispatch batching (M3 — multiple kernels per command buffer)
Pipeline cache cross-machine sharing (vendor-locked format anyway)
GPU-side timing via VkQueryPool (would need a separate milestone for perf profiling primitives)
acceptance_criteria: cargo test --workspace: ≥492 preserved + new tests for KernelHandle + pipeline cache
saxpy_1m dispatch on NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell drops from 34 ms to < 2 ms (>17× speedup)
saxpy_1m dispatch on Lavapipe drops from 4.2 ms to < 1 ms
Repeated dispatch of same kernel: second and subsequent calls at least 100× faster than cold start (proves cache works)
All 6 existing GPU tests still pass bit-exact
New baselines blessed in .pipeline/benchmarks/baselines.json; machine metadata distinguishes NVIDIA vs Lavapipe
No new SPIR-V or HIR changes (pure runtime rework)
open_questions_for_architect: KernelHandle identity: opaque struct with Arc<Device>? Or token that indexes VulkanContext's cache? Arc-based is more idiomatic Rust.
Pipeline cache path: ~/.cache/axc/pipeline.cache or env override? XDG_CACHE_HOME. Disable-able via env for CI reproducibility.
Buffer pool vs per-KernelHandle buffers: M2.3a uses per-KernelHandle (simpler). Shared pool is M3.
Copy barriers: src_stage COPY + src_access TRANSFER_WRITE → dst_stage COMPUTE_SHADER + dst_access SHADER_READ. Symmetric for readback.
Staging buffer size: exactly input size, or rounded up to power of 2 for reuse? Round up.
How does the correctness oracle (bit-exact check) interact with device-local? Still readback to host via staging, compare. Same as before just with extra copy.
Baseline re-bless: automated via `cargo bench -- --bless-m23a` or manual? Manual — AXC_BLESS_BASELINES=1 already exists.
Is pipeline cache disk load/save safe in concurrent test runs? File lock? Probably use a temp file + atomic rename; or disable in tests.
